title: Discrete-Time Signals and Systems
summary: This page introduces discrete-time signals as uniformly spaced samples of continuous-time signals, expressed using the notation x[n] = x(nT). It discusses natural occurrences of discrete-time signals in population studies, amortization, and radar tracking, as well as their use in digital filtering where continuous-time signals are processed by discrete-time systems via C/D and D/C conversion.

figures.caption: Processing continuous-time signals by discrete-time systems, where a continuous-time input x(t) is converted to a discrete-time signal x[n] via a C/D converter, processed by a discrete-time system to yield y[n], and then converted back to a continuous-time output y(t) via a D/C converter.
